title: Indus Valley Religious Culture
description: Some elements later associated with Hindu traditions are often discussed alongside the Indus Valley civilization, though direct continuity is debated.

title: Early Vedic Tradition
description: The early Vedic period begins, laying foundations for later Hindu religious thought and practice.

title: Rigveda Composed
description: The Rigveda, the oldest Vedic text, takes shape over a long period in the late second and early first millennium BCE.

title: Later Vedic Period
description: Brahmana texts and expanding ritual traditions characterize the later Vedic age.

title: Upanishads Emerge
description: The oldest Upanishads appear, emphasizing philosophical reflection on brahman, atman, and liberation.

title: Interaction with Shramana Traditions
description: Hindu thought develops in dialogue and competition with Buddhism, Jainism, and other shramana movements.

title: Epics Take Shape
description: The Mahabharata and Ramayana develop over centuries and become central to Hindu religious culture.

title: Bhagavad Gita Composed
description: The Bhagavad Gita is likely composed around the 1st or 2nd century CE.

title: Puranic Hinduism Expands
description: Puranic traditions, temple worship, and the major devotional cults of Vishnu, Shiva, and Devi grow in influence.

title: Gupta Era Flourishing
description: Under the Guptas, classical Sanskrit culture and major Hindu traditions flourish.

title: Temple Traditions Flourish
description: From late antiquity into the early medieval period, major temple-centered forms of Hindu worship expand.

title: Adi Shankara and Advaita Vedanta
description: Adi Shankara's life and commentarial tradition become foundational for Advaita Vedanta.

title: Ramanuja's Vishishtadvaita
description: Ramanuja helps shape one of the most influential Vaishnava philosophical traditions.

title: Bhakti Movement Expands
description: Devotional movements spread across India in many vernacular languages.

title: Vijayanagara Patronage
description: The Vijayanagara Empire becomes a major center of Hindu royal patronage in South India.

title: Chaitanya and Gaudiya Vaishnavism
description: Chaitanya becomes a central figure in the devotional tradition later known as Gaudiya Vaishnavism.

title: Brahmo Samaj Founded
description: Rammohun Roy helps launch the Brahmo Samaj in 1828, beginning an influential reform movement.

title: Vivekananda at Chicago
description: Swami Vivekananda's 1893 Parliament of the World's Religions speech introduces Hindu ideas to a major global audience.

title: Hindu Traditions in Modern India
description: After 1947, Hindu traditions continue to evolve within the independent Republic of India and the wider diaspora.

title: ISKCON Founded
description: The International Society for Krishna Consciousness is founded in 1966 and spreads globally.
